1. Install the `@sentry/nextjs` package

---

Sentry error logging has been successfully added to the project. Here's what was set up:

**Files created:**
- `sentry.client.config.ts` - Client-side Sentry initialization with session replay
- `sentry.server.config.ts` - Server-side Sentry initialization
- `sentry.edge.config.ts` - Edge runtime Sentry initialization
- `src/instrumentation.ts` - Next.js instrumentation hook for server/edge initialization
- `src/app/error.tsx` - App-level error boundary that reports to Sentry
- `src/app/global-error.tsx` - Global error boundary for root layout errors

**Files modified:**
- `next.config.ts` - Wrapped with `withSentryConfig` for source map uploads
- `env.example` - Added Sentry environment variables

**Environment variables to configure:**
```
NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN=     # Your Sentry DSN
SENTRY_ORG=                 # Your Sentry organization slug
SENTRY_PROJECT=             # Your Sentry project slug
SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN=          # Auth token for source map uploads
```

You can get your DSN from your Sentry project settings under **Client Keys (DSN)**. The auth token can be generated at https://sentry.io/settings/auth-tokens/.

---
